description: Tomatoes grow well in warm climates with full sunlight and regular watering. Rich loamy soil improves yield.
Wheat prefers cool weather and well-drained fertile soil. Moderate irrigation gives best yield.
Rice thrives in humid, warm conditions and requires standing water throughout most of its growth.
Maize grows best in hot weather and well-drained fertile soil with regular irrigation.
Potatoes prefer cool weather and loose loamy soil with consistent moisture.
Onions need full sun, light loamy soil, and consistent watering.
Garlic grows in cool climates and needs loose, fertile soil for bulb formation.
Cabbage grows best in cool climates and rich soil with ample water.
Cauliflower prefers cool weather and fertile soil with regular watering.
Spinach grows throughout the year with proper watering and shade.
